plan: 1. Define the ContentModule interface and module registry type in `src/domain/types.ts`. Add: `ContentModule { id: string; label: string; compatibleArchetypeIds: readonly string[]; compatibleEraIds: readonly string[]; render(site: GeneratedSite, rng: SeededRandom): HTMLElement }` and a `ContentModuleId` union type.

2. Create content dataset file `src/domain/data/module-fragments.ts` with curated text pools per module — e.g. feature names/descriptions for features-grid, fake headlines/dates for news-block, testimonial quotes/names for testimonials, download labels/versions for download-panel, guild member names/ranks for guild-roster, route pairs for route-map, and tier names/prices/features for pricing-table. Each pool should have 10–15 entries to ensure variety across seeds.

3. Implement at least 6 content module renderers in `src/render/modules/`, one file per module: (a) `render-features-grid.ts` — 3–6 feature cards with icon placeholder, title, description; (b) `render-news-block.ts` — 3–5 fake news items with date, headline, snippet; (c) `render-download-panel.ts` — download button, version info, system requirements; (d) `render-testimonials.ts` — 2–4 fake testimonials with quote and attribution; (e) `render-guild-roster.ts` — table of 5–8 members with name, rank, join date; (f) `render-pricing-table.ts` — 2–3 tier columns with name, price, bullet features, CTA. Each renderer uses `deriveSeed(site.seed, moduleId)` + `createRng()` for deterministic content selection and consumes CSS custom properties from the active theme.

4. Create the module registry `src/render/modules/index.ts` that exports a `contentModules` array of `ContentModule` objects. Each entry declares its `id`, `compatibleArchetypeIds` (e.g. guild-roster only for mmorpg-guild; pricing-table for e-commerce-shop, webmail-provider, os-vendor; features-grid for all), and `compatibleEraIds`. The registry is the single source of truth for module selection.

5. Implement module selection logic in a new file `src/render/select-modules.ts`: `selectModules(site: GeneratedSite): ContentModule[]`. Filter `contentModules` by archetype + era compatibility, then use `createRng(deriveSeed(site.seed, 'modules'))` to shuffle compatible modules and pick 2–4 (with `rng.int(2, 4)`). This guarantees determinism and at least 2 modules per page.

6. Wire modules into `src/render/render-homepage.ts`: import `selectModules`, call it after renderHero, create a `<main class="site-body">` wrapper, append each selected module's `render()` result into it, and insert the `<main>` between the hero and footer in the DOM.

7. Add CSS for all modules in `src/styles/generated-site.css` (or a new `src/styles/modules.css` imported from `generated-site.css`). All styles must use CSS custom properties (--color-primary, --border-radius, --spacing-medium, etc.) so modules automatically follow the active theme. Add responsive rules for mobile layout (single-column at narrow widths).

8. Add German vocabulary overrides in module renderers where text is user-visible (e.g. 'Features' → 'Funktionen', 'Download' → 'Herunterladen', 'Pricing' → 'Preise') by reading `site.region.vocabularyOverrides`.

9. Write tests in `tests/us-011-content-modules.test.ts` covering: (a) at least 6 module files exist; (b) selectModules returns 2–4 modules for any seed; (c) determinism — same seed always returns same modules in same order; (d) different seeds produce different module selections; (e) archetype compatibility — guild-roster only appears for mmorpg-guild seeds; (f) rendered DOM contains `.site-body` with child module sections; (g) each module renders valid HTML with expected class names; (h) modules use theme CSS variables (spot-check); (i) smoke test across 20+ seeds with no errors; (j) snapshot stability for 2–3 pinned seeds.
10. Run `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` to verify all checks pass. Fix any issues.
testing_recommendations: Determinism: For 10+ fixed seeds, assert selectModules returns identical module IDs in identical order across repeated calls
Compatibility filtering: Seed the RNG to produce each archetype and verify guild-roster never appears for non-mmorpg archetypes, route-map never appears for non-airline archetypes, etc.
DOM structure: Assert rendered homepage contains header > hero > main.site-body > footer in that order, and main.site-body has >= 2 child sections
Theme adherence: For each era, render a module and assert key elements reference CSS custom properties (check inline styles or computed styles in jsdom)
Cross-seed variation: Generate 20 sites and assert at least 3 distinct module-selection sets appear (guards against accidentally always picking the same modules)
Snapshot regression: Pin 3 seeds (one early-web, one startup-minimalism, one alt-timeline) and snapshot the full innerHTML of .site-body for regression detection
Regional overrides: Generate a de-de site and assert German labels appear in module headings where overrides are defined
Edge case: If only 2 modules are compatible for a given archetype+era combo, verify exactly 2 are selected (no crash from rng.int requesting more than available)
risks: Module count floor: If a rare archetype+era combination has fewer than 2 compatible modules, selectModules must clamp to available count rather than crash. The selection logic needs a Math.min guard.
RNG isolation: New module-selection RNG stream must use deriveSeed(seed, 'modules') to avoid perturbing existing header/hero/footer RNG sequences — otherwise snapshot tests from US-010 will break.

Dataset size vs. repetition: With only 8 archetypes and 6 modules, some archetypes may always get the same 2 modules. Ensuring enough compatible modules per archetype (at least 3–4 universal ones like features-grid, news-block) mitigates this.

CSS specificity conflicts: Module styles must not clash with existing hero/header/footer styles. Namespace all module classes under .site-body or use module-specific prefixes (e.g. .module-features-grid).

Bundle size: 6 module renderers + dataset file adds code. Keep datasets lean (no large prose blocks) and rely on template interpolation with site metadata to create variety without bloating the bundle.
